expectations.text: Identifies the consent-model difference: CCPA is opt-out (sale/sharing) vs GDPR opt-in lawful basis; no CCPA requirement for a processing legal basis
expectations.evidence: 'CCPA is opt-out (for sale/sharing); GDPR requires an opt-in lawful basis for processing generally. In fact, CCPA has no lawful-basis requirement at all — businesses can collect PI without needing to establish one of GDPR's six lawful bases.'

expectations.text: Identifies CCPA-specific mechanics GDPR programmes lack: 'Do Not Sell or Share' link, GPC honoring, right to limit sensitive PI use, notice at collection specifics, service provider/contractor contract terms under §1798.100(d)/.140
expectations.evidence: Lists 'Do Not Sell or Share' link, 'Honor GPC signals', 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information' link, notice at collection '(§1798.100(a)-(b))' as a distinct artifact, and vendor contracts 'meeting §1798.100(d) required terms'. Directly cites the required section.

expectations.text: Notes scope differences: CCPA covers households, has revenue/volume applicability thresholds, and its 'sale' concept is broader than anything in GDPR
expectations.evidence: 'CCPA protects consumers and households, not just identified/identifiable individuals as under GDPR', 'Only businesses meeting the revenue/volume applicability thresholds are covered by CCPA — GDPR has no thresholds', 'CCPA's "sale" concept... is broader than anything in GDPR.'

expectations.text: Notes rights differences accurately (e.g., CCPA has no full GDPR-style portability/objection regime; correction was added by CPRA; statutory damages private right of action only for data breaches, $100-$750 per consumer per incident)
expectations.evidence: 'The right to correct was only added by CPRA (§1798.106)', 'CCPA has no GDPR-style objection right and no full portability regime', 'CCPA's private right of action is limited to data breaches — statutory damages of $100–$750 per consumer per incident (§1798.150), class-action eligible.'

expectations.text: Provides a concrete delta-plan (map data flows for sale/sharing, implement opt-out signals, update notices, remediate vendor contracts, train request-handling to CCPA deadlines)
expectations.evidence: 'Practical Build Plan' lists 8 concrete steps: standalone notice at collection, opt-out/limit-SPI links with 15-day SLA, vendor contract audit flagging CCBA third parties, right-to-correct workflow, minors opt-in, annual threshold review, risk-assessment applicability, ADMT deadline planning.
